notes: The diagram is partially readable with most task labels legible, but the image is cropped at the bottom cutting off several steps and gateway branches. The left swimlane area contains overlapping control annotations that obscure flow arrows, and several referenced subprocess boxes in the upper-left cluster are small and densely packed making individual labels difficult to confirm with certainty. The right-side Customer lane is clearer but also cut off at the bottom. This is a tile/crop of a larger diagram, meaning multiple flows are cut off at the edges (left, right, top, bottom), making it impossible to trace complete end-to-end paths. Most task labels within the visible area are legible, but several gateway branch labels are small and partially obscured, and the dense clustering of elements in the centre-left creates overlapping annotation text that competes with flow arrows. The right side of the diagram is cut off mid-process, and several dashed-line flows exit the frame without clear destinations. (Tiles: Tile 1: 62% · Tile 2: 52% · Tile 3: 62% · Tile 4: 62% · Tile 5: 62% · Tile 6: 28%)
